misconception: Complexity and length are the same thing on the SAT.
why_incorrect: Complexity and length are two distinct quality indicators assessed separately on the WSET SAT. Complexity refers to the range and variety of flavour and aroma types simultaneously present — a wine can show citrus, stone fruit, floral, mineral, and oak-derived notes simultaneously, making it complex. Length (or finish) refers to the persistence of flavour sensations after the wine has been swallowed or spat — how long the flavour lingers. A wine can have a long finish with limited complexity (one note sustained), or high complexity with a short finish. Both are assessed in the BICL framework (Balance, Intensity, Complexity, Length) and contribute separately to quality assessment.
corrected_understanding: Complexity = range of flavour and aroma types present simultaneously. Length = duration of flavour after swallowing. These are separate criteria in SAT quality assessment and must be addressed independently.
detection_signals: complexity and length are the same thing
doesn't length mean how complex the wine is
if a wine is complex it must also have a long finish
I wrote complexity because the finish was long
length means how many flavours you get which is the same as complexity

coaching_content.confusion_statement: You seem to be confusing: Complexity and length are the same thing on the SAT.
coaching_content.evidence_statement: This is based on your answers showing this pattern.
coaching_content.improvement_signal: Correctly explaining the relationship in similar questions
